title: Rooftop sunset mixer
description: Small group, curated intros, zero awkward icebreakers. Drinks provided.
moreAbout: We keep the group intentionally small so you can actually talk — think short rotations, curated intros, and a sunset view. The host opens with a light structure so you’re never stuck in awkward silence. Non-alcoholic options are always available.
allowedAndNotes: Outdoor terrace: dress for wind and evening chill. Small bags OK; large luggage should stay downstairs. Smoking only in designated areas off the deck.

dos: Bring a photo ID if you’re drinking alcohol
Stay for at least the first rotation so everyone gets a fair intro
Use headphones if you need to take a call — step to the stairwell
donts: No glass near the railing — use the cups provided
Don’t invite plus-ones who aren’t ticketed
No streaming or live broadcasting without host approval

faqs.q: What’s the vibe — networking or party?
faqs.a: Social, not salesy. You’ll meet people in short, structured rounds with time to go deeper if you click.
We move to the covered lounge on the same floor — same start time, same host flow.
This prototype doesn’t process payments — in production you’d see the host’s cancellation window here.

title: Founders breakfast club
description: Early birds only. Share what you're building and one ask for the table.
moreAbout: A tight table for builders who want signal over noise. Everyone shares a one-minute update, then one concrete “ask” — feedback, intro, or a specific challenge. No slides unless the host opens the floor.
whatsIncluded: Timed agenda run by the host
allowedAndNotes: Laptops closed during intros; you can open them during the optional working block at the end. Dietary notes: oat milk and fruit available — flag allergies when you join.

dos: Come with one clear ask for the group
Respect the timer — it keeps the room fair
Share contact info only when both sides opt in
donts: No pitching investors cold in the breakfast round
Don’t record audio or video of other attendees
No recruiting spam — use the opt-in sheet if hiring

faqs.q: Is this only for funded startups?
faqs.a: No — early ideas and side projects are welcome as long as you’re serious about showing up prepared.
This session is in person so the table stays intimate and on time.

title: Gallery night walk
description: Three stops, one coffee break, great for solo attendees.
moreAbout: We visit three small galleries in one evening with a guided thread — no art degree required. There’s a midpoint coffee stop so the pace stays relaxed. Solo attendees are the norm; the host breaks the ice at stop one.
allowedAndNotes: Photography follows each gallery’s rules — when in doubt, ask. Comfortable shoes; we walk ~2 km total. Large backpacks should be left at coat check where available.

dos: Stay with the group between stops so no one’s left behind
Keep voices low inside exhibition rooms
Tip baristas if you order extra beyond the included drink
donts: No touching artwork or leaning on plinths
Don’t run late into a gallery mid-tour — catch us at the next stop

faqs.q: Do I need to know about art?
faqs.a: No — the host shares just enough context to enjoy each stop.
Meet us at the second stop — we’ll share the walking route in the booking message.

title: Weekend board games
description: Beginner-friendly. Snacks, soft drinks, and a few competitive souls.
moreAbout: Low-pressure tabletop afternoon: we teach rules as we go and split by experience so no one gets steamrolled. Snacks and soft drinks are on the house; BYO favorite game if you want it on the table.
whatsIncluded: Table assignments by vibe (cozy vs competitive)
allowedAndNotes: Vegetarian snacks by default — tell the host about allergies when you join. Shoes off at the door; socks or indoor slippers welcome.

dos: Wash hands before handling shared cards and pieces
Call the host if a rule argument breaks the fun
Help reset the table when a game ends
donts: No food or drinks over the boards
Don’t bend cards or slam pieces
No outside alcohol — keep it soft drinks here

faqs.q: I’ve never played these games — OK?
faqs.a: Yes — that’s the default. We’ll place you with a patient table captain.
This session is 18+ unless the listing says otherwise.

title: Harbor-side jog + coffee
description: Easy 5k, then espresso. Pace groups at signup.
moreAbout: We split into pace groups at the meetup point, jog an easy loop, then regroup for espresso nearby. Nobody gets left behind — there’s always a sweep host at the back.
allowedAndNotes: Wear visible gear if you run before sunrise. Headphones OK on the path but keep one ear open near roads. Bring ID if you plan to order extra after the run.

dos: Sign the safety waiver in the booking flow
Stick with your pace group unless you tell the sweep host
Stretch at the finish before heading to coffee
donts: No racing cars or weaving into traffic
Don’t leave the route without telling the host
No litter — carry out what you bring in

faqs.a: Roughly 6:30–7:30 min/km for the main group; a slower group leaves five minutes earlier on a shorter loop.
We cancel two hours ahead and message everyone — check your email the night before.
